what_it_does: TypeScript type declarations for the `Card` component: declares a `CardProps` interface extending `React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`, the `Card` function signature, and default export. Documents variants and modifiers via JSDoc.
observations: `variant` union: 'soft' | 'surface' | 'outline' | 'gold', described as soft (cream deck panel), surface (white content card), outline (stroke only), gold (gold-tinted).
standouts: Carries optional structured slots (title/sub/footer) in addition to children, making it a container with built-in header/footer regions.
conventions: JSDoc variant descriptions tie classes to product surface semantics (cream deck panel, white content card).
concerns: `as?: any` loosens polymorphic-element type safety (no element/component constraint).
notes: The type contract for Card: variant (soft/surface/outline/gold) + raised/interactive/pad + title/sub/footer slots, extending React div attributes.
questions: Does the .jsx render all four variants and the title/sub/footer slots as documented?
purpose_vs_actual: Match â€” it is a type-declaration companion accurately describing Card's props.
intention_signals: Aims to give consumers a documented, IDE-discoverable type surface for the container primitive including its header/footer slot regions.
novelty: Slot props (title/sub/footer) typed at the container level rather than left entirely to children composition.
contribution: Contributes the typed prop vocabulary for the surface/container primitive â€” the variant taxonomy (soft/surface/outline/gold) plus raised/interactive/pad and the title/sub/footer slot contract.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript .d.ts declaration for a Card component defining a variant union (soft/surface/outline/gold), raised/interactive/pad modifiers, and title/sub/footer slots, extending React div attributes.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a typed, documented contract for the Card container primitive including its variants and header/footer slots.
